reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The conversion resulted in the total loss of approximately 34 pages of content. Content from pages 6-8 (FCC NPRM Background and Fact Sheet), pages 10-33 (FCC NPRM main text), and pages 35-42 (House Bill 213, NG911 Migration Process, NMAC, and WA 911 Jobs marketing) is entirely absent. The element index jumps (e.g., from 46 to 87 and 96 to 438) confirm that vast sections were dropped during conversion.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 6-8 (FCC NPRM Background/Fact Sheet), 10-33 (NPRM text), and 35-42 (HB 213, NG911 Migration, NMAC info, WA Jobs marketing).
concerns.output_quote: Entire sections and pages are missing. Indices jump from 46 directly to 87, 96 to 438, and 450 to 556.
concerns.location: Sections following element indices 46, 96, and 450; corresponding to pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42.

concerns.analysis: The invoice tables on pages 45 and 46 suffer from major row loss. While the source document includes nested detail rows for each line item (detailing specific air times and spot IDs), the output table in element 585 only includes the summary rows. Line 1 in the source has 5 detail rows, and Line 2 has 6 detail rows, all of which are missing in the summary-only output table.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 45 Invoice table includes 5 detail rows for Line 1, 6 detail rows for Line 2, and 3 detail rows for Line 3.
concerns.output_quote: Element 585 only contains the summary rows for Lines 1, 2, and 3, omitting more than 20% of the data rows.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: A significant text omission occurs in the FCC NPRM Introduction on page 9. Element 95 captures only the first sentence of the introduction and cuts off mid-sentence, losing the entire remainder of the paragraph and the section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 9, Section I Introduction, Paragraph 1: '...Section 902 of the new legislation requires the Commission to take action to help address the diversion of 911 fees by states and other jurisdictions for purposes unrelated to 911.'
concerns.output_quote: Element 95: '...Section 902 of the new legislation requires the'

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: Several minor text errors were found that change the meaning or provide incorrect information. In House Bill 203 (element 446), the conversion failed to recognize the strikethrough on '2021', resulting in '2021, 2022' which implies both dates are relevant. Additionally, in the Contract table (elements 559, 561, 562), several fields are truncated (e.g., 'Murra' for 'Murray', 'BI' for 'Blue', and '10-1' for '10-11P').
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 34, HB 203 Section 1: 'Effective July 1, ~~2021~~, 2022,'. Page 43 Contract Table: 'Murray', 'Blue', '10-11P'.

concerns.analysis: The numbered agenda items in the meeting minutes (e.g., items 1 through 11 across pages 2-5) have been converted into unordered bullet lists. Furthermore, the explicit sequence numbers (e.g., '1.', '2.', etc.) have been removed from the start of the text content within the list items, leading to a loss of sequence information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 1. Chair Opening Remarks – Mr. Greene kicked off the meeting welcoming the group. (Page 2, Agenda item 1)
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Chair Opening Remarks – Mr. Greene kicked off the meeting welcoming the group."

concerns.analysis: Visible URLs and hyperlinked text in the source document (e.g., FCC website references, the Sinclair Broadcast Group URL on page 44) are rendered as plain text in the JSON output rather than interactive link objects.

action_items: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.
